title: Magic numbers 100 and 10 in lichen_lora_l2_stop/deinit join timeouts
description:
lora_l2.c uses unexplained timeout constants:

Line 376: `k_thread_join(&rx_thread_data, K_MSEC(RX_THREAD_QUICK_JOIN_MS));`
- RX_THREAD_QUICK_JOIN_MS is defined at line 46 as 100

Line 427: `k_thread_join(&rx_thread_data, K_MSEC(10));`
- Hardcoded 10ms with inline comment explaining rationale

The 100ms value at line 46 has no comment explaining why 100ms was chosen. The comment at lines 369-375 explains it's 'ample time for a thread that checked running between lora_recv() calls' but doesn't justify the specific value.

The 10ms at line 427 has good inline explanation.

What goes wrong: A maintainer tuning timeouts cannot verify if 100ms is tight or has headroom. 

Improvement: Either document the 100ms derivation (e.g., 'worst-case context switch time on ARM M4 at 64MHz') or make it a Kconfig with help text.
